context: Relief-rally tape with a live catch. June CPI landed soft this morning — headline -0.4% on tumbling gasoline, core flat vs +0.2% expected, core YoY 2.6% vs 2.8% — and the rates market repriced hard: July hike odds collapsed to ~12% from near 50%, lifting BTC to ~$63.8k. The catch: hawkish new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh testifies before the House at 10am ET today (Senate Banking Jul 15), and his '2% or bust' framework could walk back the dovish read — which is why Fear & Greed still sits at 22 (Extreme Fear) even as spot rallies. Bias across BTC / XRP / SUI / SOL is NEUTRAL-to-cautiously-constructive: the CPI print gives the bulls a lane, but do not chase into Warsh — let the testimony clear before adding, and mind the reignited Iran conflict as an oil / geopolitical tail.

tickers.thesis: XRP is +2.9% to ~$1.10 on the macro relief (soft June CPI, July hike odds → ~12%), but that is a borrowed rally — the coin's own binary, the CLARITY Act, is jammed in the Senate short of votes on three fights (Trump's crypto income, DeFi rules, stablecoin rewards), with the floor vote needing to clear before the August recess (odds ~40-50%). Note XRP is still -2.4% on the week even after today's pop. Bias NEUTRAL with a downside skew on legislative headlines: long only on a sweep-and-reclaim of the $1.055 24h low with momentum; sub-$1 is live if CLARITY stalls, and a daily close below $1.05 invalidates.
levels: Psychological — sub-$1 risk on CLARITY stall

setup.entry: Long the sweep-and-reclaim of $1.055 into the $1.07-1.08 OTE (70.5%), NY killzone momentum only
setup.invalidation: Daily close < $1.05; or a CLARITY stall / shelved-bill headline

catalysts: CLARITY Act — jammed in the Senate on 3 fights (Trump crypto income, DeFi rules, stablecoin rewards); floor vote needed before the Aug recess, odds ~40-50%. THE binary. [Regulatory-US]
Macro relief — soft June CPI (Jul 14) cut July hike odds to ~12%; lifts the whole complex but is not CLARITY progress. [Macro-Risk]
XRP spot ETFs — ~$1.44B cumulative; Standard Chartered models $4-8B first-year IF CLARITY passes. [ETF & Flows]

insights.title: Sentiment deepening: F&G 22 (Extreme Fear), Δ -6 vs yesterday
insights.body: Crypto Fear & Greed Index printed 22 (Extreme Fear) at 13:49Z — -6 vs yesterday's reading. F&G under 30 historically aligns with bounce-watch zones; under 20 with capitulation. Combined with the recent price action and live headlines (see LIVE FEED), the regime is risk-off until a sweep-and-reclaim candle prints. No new longs until F&G stops falling AND BTC reclaims its 24h high.

insights.title: Today's macro releases (auto from ForexFactory)
insights.body: USD high-impact releases registered today: ✓ Core CPI m/m → forecast 0.2% | prev 0.2%; ✓ Core CPI y/y → forecast 2.8% | prev 2.9%; ✓ CPI m/m → forecast -0.1% | prev 0.5%. Live event data pulled from ForexFactory weekly JSON at 13:49Z.

insights.title: Soft CPI opened a lane — Warsh can close it
insights.body: June CPI came in dovish (core YoY 2.6% vs 2.8% exp) and July hike odds cratered to ~12% from near 50%, giving BTC a lane to $63.8k. But hawkish Fed Chair Warsh testifies before the House at 10am ET today, and his June debut already killed the market's cut thesis. This is a relief rally on loan until Warsh confirms or reverses it — don't chase the gap.

insights.title: The rally the tape doesn't believe
insights.body: BTC is +2.5% to $63.8k, yet Fear & Greed FELL to 22 (Δ -6) on the same day. Sentiment is not buying the bounce — a classic disbelief-rally divergence. It needs a $64.2k reclaim that holds through Warsh to convert doubt into trend; a failed reclaim invites a fade back into the range.

insights.title: 'CPI dip masks core' is the trap
insights.body: The headline dropped mostly on gasoline; core inflation cooled to 2.6% YoY but is still above the Fed's 2% target. One soft print is not a trend, and a hawkish Warsh can reframe it in a single sentence — which is exactly the asymmetric risk into the 10am ET testimony.

insights.title: XRP's clock is running out
insights.body: CLARITY is stuck in the Senate on three fights — Trump's crypto income, DeFi rules, stablecoin rewards — with a pre-August floor-vote deadline and odds ~40-50%. Today's XRP pop is macro-driven, not CLARITY progress; the coin is still -2.4% on the week, and sub-$1 risk stays live on any stall headline. Trade the level ($1.055 reclaim), not the story.

insights.title: SUI leads, SOL lags — the L1-rotation tell
insights.body: SUI is +4.4% 24h / +2.2% 7d; SOL is +2.5% 24h but -5.2% 7d. Money is favoring SUI's ending unlock-cliff + ETF pipeline over SOL's inflow-absorbed-by-unlocks paradox. In a relief bounce, relative strength is the tell — SUI is the cleaner long-on-reclaim, SOL the cleaner fade.

hidden_signals.body: Fear & Greed at 22 (Extreme Fear), Δ -6 vs yesterday — sub-30 zones historically align with bounce setups but require a sweep-and-reclaim candle to confirm
HackerNews trending crypto-tagged: "Building and shipping Mac and iOS apps without opening Xcode" (↑516)
July rate-hike odds collapsed to ~12% (from near 50%) within minutes of the soft CPI — the clearest signal of the day, and it lives in the rates market, not on any crypto chart.

Fed Chair Warsh testifies before House Financial Services at 10am ET TODAY (Senate Banking Jul 15) — a live 'CPI-plus-Warsh' collision that can reverse the relief rally; watch for any '2% or bust' hawkish reframe.
Fear & Greed 22 (Extreme Fear), Δ -6 vs yesterday even as BTC rallied +2.5% — sentiment is not buying the bounce; classic disbelief-rally divergence.
Core CPI cooled (YoY 2.6% vs 2.8% exp) but the headline drop was gasoline-led — 'the CPI dip masks core inflation risk'; the disinflation trend is not confirmed on one print.

CLARITY Act jammed on three specific fights (Trump's crypto income, DeFi rules, stablecoin rewards); Senate returned Jul 13 with a pre-August window — a legislative gauge the XRP price feed does not show.
Iran conflict reignited — an oil / geopolitical tail that can undo the very disinflation read the CPI just delivered.
L1 rotation under the surface: SUI (+2.2% 7d) is outperforming SOL (-5.2% 7d) — a relative-strength shift the headline market-cap ranking obscures.
